settings.autoSaveHelp: YAT saves a moment after you stop typing. Press Ctrl+S or Cmd+S if you want to force a save.
settings.autoSaveFailedDetailBody: Your changes are still on this screen and have not been written locally yet. Fix the issue, then try again. Reason: {{error}}
settings.validationNoticeBody: Check the General tab for details.
settings.closeSaveBlocked: Could not close yet because some settings still need attention

overview.title: Get YAT ready to use
overview.desc: Set up speech, output, and the permissions that matter first. Once those feel solid, every recording becomes easier to trust.

summary.speechPending: Finish the STT endpoint and model setup first.
summary.polishPending: Polishing is enabled, but the LLM setup is still incomplete.
summary.polishOff: Raw transcription is being used directly right now.
summary.outputAutoPaste: The result will be pasted into the current cursor position.
summary.outputClipboard: The result will only be copied to the clipboard.

setup.title: Start with these three checks
setup.desc: Handle the biggest experience drivers first: speech setup, output mode, and your text rules.
setup.speechPending: The STT endpoint and model are not fully set up yet.
setup.outputAutoPaste: Results will be pasted into the current cursor position.
setup.outputClipboard: Results will only be copied to the clipboard.
setup.promptReady: You already have {{count}} vocabulary rules or custom instructions.
setup.promptPending: You are still using the default rules, which is fine until you need more control.
setup.promptOptional: Polishing is off, so prompt work can wait.

permissions.title: Platform limits & permissions
permissions.desc: Microphone access, hotkeys, and auto-paste behave differently on each platform. Knowing the differences makes troubleshooting much easier.
permissions.macosMicrophoneBody: If microphone access was denied earlier, reopen it under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone and allow YAT again.
permissions.macosAccessibilityBody: If you want auto-paste to work, YAT usually also needs to be allowed under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility.
permissions.windowsMicrophoneBody: Open Settings → Privacy & security → Microphone and confirm desktop apps are allowed to use the mic.
permissions.windowsAutoPasteBody: If the target app runs elevated and YAT does not, simulated paste can fail. Clipboard-only mode is usually the safer fallback.
permissions.linuxMicrophoneBody: Make sure your desktop environment, packaging format, or portal setup allows microphone access.
permissions.linuxHotkeyBody: YAT currently relies on an X11-style global hotkey listener. In native Wayland sessions, hotkeys may fail completely unless your desktop environment provides compatible support.
permissions.unknownBody: If recording, hotkeys, or paste feels unreliable, check microphone, keyboard monitoring, and accessibility-related permissions first.
permissions.waylandBody: YAT currently depends on X11-style global hotkeys, so support under native Wayland may be limited. Try XWayland, or check whether your desktop environment offers global shortcut support.
permissions.openSettingsFailed: Could not open system settings directly. Please follow the path shown in the card and open it manually.

recent.title: Latest result
recent.desc: This area shows the most recent pipeline state so you can quickly see where things stopped.
recent.emptyDesc: Record a short sample and the newest result will appear here.

prompt.title: Current prompt & vocabulary rules
prompt.desc: The prompt page combines the system prompt, your custom instructions, and vocabulary rules so you can inspect what is actually sent.
prompt.enabledHint: These rules currently shape the polished output.
prompt.disabledHint: Polishing is off, so these rules are currently inactive.

pasteFailSuggest.title: Auto-paste keeps failing
pasteFailSuggest.body: Recent auto-paste attempts have all fallen back to the clipboard. If you want something steadier, switch to clipboard-only mode.

general.singleDesc: Press once to start, press again to stop.
general.doubleTapDesc: Trigger by hitting the same key twice quickly.
general.comboDesc: Requires a modifier, so it is harder to trigger by accident.
general.holdDesc: Only record while the key is held down.
general.keyHint: Examples: RCtrl, RCmd, Alt, F8, Space, A, Comma, Slash. Use the interactive recorder for best results.
general.modifierHint: Examples: Ctrl, Alt, Shift, or Cmd.
general.doubleTapHint: Shorter is harder to trigger accidentally, but also demands faster tapping.
general.hotkeyPreview: You will use {{hotkey}} to trigger recording.
general.hotkeyBehaviorHint: All trigger modes stay available, so you can start with the one that feels best and adjust later.
general.hotkeyRecommendedBody: Hold-to-talk on {{recommended}} is usually the safest default. It keeps accidental triggers low and stays out of the foreground app's way.
general.hotkeyComboBody: If you do not want hold-to-talk, a combo is usually the next most reliable choice.
general.hotkeyModifierHoldBody: Hold-to-talk on Alt / Ctrl / Cmd is supported, but those keys sit closer to app and OS shortcuts than {{recommended}}. Test it in the apps you actually use before depending on it.
general.hotkeyModifierRiskBody: Single or double-tap modifier keys can affect app menus, OS shortcuts, or focus behavior before YAT knows what you intended. Prefer hold-to-talk on {{recommended}} or a combo instead.
general.hotkeyTypingRiskTitle: This hotkey will interfere with typing
general.hotkeyTypingRiskBody: Using letters, Space, Tab, or Enter as a standalone hotkey steals normal typing. Keep it only if that is truly what you want.
general.hotkeyCustomBody: This setup can work, but you should try it in the apps you actually use. The safest patterns are still hold-to-talk on {{recommended}} or a combo you never need for typing.
general.hotkeyWindowPauseBody: When the settings window is focused, YAT pauses recording hotkeys automatically so editing fields does not accidentally start dictation.
general.recordingHotkeyBody: Hold one key to set hold-to-talk, tap twice for double-tap, press two keys together for a combo, or do a short press for single-tap.
general.recordingWaitingKeys: Waiting for key input (press Esc to cancel)
general.hotkeyValidationMissingKey: Enter a key name first. Escape is reserved for cancel, so choose another key.
general.hotkeyValidationEscapeReserved: Escape is reserved for cancelling recordings and cannot be used as the hotkey or modifier.
general.hotkeyValidationUnsupportedKey: Use a supported key name: letters, digits, F1–F12, arrow keys, punctuation keys, modifier keys, navigation keys (Home / End / Insert / Delete / Page Up / Page Down, etc.) or numpad keys.
general.hotkeyValidationMissingModifier: Combo mode needs both a main key and a modifier.
general.hotkeyValidationUnsupportedModifier: Use a supported modifier name such as Ctrl, Alt, Shift, Cmd, or another supported single key.
general.hotkeyValidationSameKey: The combo key and modifier must be different.
general.hotkeyValidationDoubleTapRange: Double-tap interval must stay between 100ms and 1000ms.
general.autoStartHint: Let YAT wait in the background as soon as you sign in.
general.startMinimizedHint: Launch hidden in the system tray instead of showing the main window.
general.closeToTrayHint: Closing the window hides YAT to the system tray instead of quitting.
general.maxRecordingHint: Lower values prevent runaway recordings; higher values work better for long dictation.
general.autoPasteDesc: Fastest when it works, but it relies on the target app accepting simulated paste.
general.clipboardOnlyDesc: More conservative and less sensitive to permission issues.
general.clipboardBehaviorHint: Decide whether a copy should always remain available after paste attempts.
general.alwaysCopyToggleHint: Keep a copy in the clipboard even when paste succeeds.
general.timeoutHint: Cancel the request if it still has not finished after this long.
general.maxRetriesHint: How many times to retry automatically after failure.
general.microphoneHint: If you just plugged in a device, refresh once before checking again.
general.soundEffectsHint: Play a sound on start, stop, or failure so you do not need to watch the screen.
general.autoMuteHint: Helps reduce feedback, but changes how you can listen to other apps while dictating.
general.autoMuteLinuxBody: Auto-mute is currently a no-op on Linux in this build. YAT still records normally, but it will not mute or restore system audio for you.
general.quickStartDesc: Most people only need the right hotkey, output mode, and microphone to get started comfortably.
general.hotkeyDesc: Choose a hotkey that is unlikely to misfire and will not get in your way.
general.recordingDesc: Adjust the microphone, recording length, and audio cues so every start and stop feels predictable.
general.outputDesc: Choose whether the result should paste immediately or wait in the clipboard.
general.platformHelpMac: On macOS, microphone and Accessibility permissions are the two most common reasons for broken paste or unreliable background hotkeys.
general.platformHelpWindows: On Windows, auto-paste often fails when the target app runs with higher privileges. Clipboard-only is usually the safer fallback.
general.platformHelpLinux: Linux desktop environments vary a lot in hotkey and simulated keyboard support, so optimize for stability first.
general.platformHelpUnknown: If hotkeys, recording, or paste feels unstable, check system-level permissions before changing app settings.
general.appearanceDesc: Theme and language now live in the top bar.
general.appearanceQuickToggleTitle: Theme & language live in the top bar
general.appearanceQuickToggleBody: Use the top-right controls to switch theme and interface language from any settings tab.
general.advancedDesc: Adjust the app for slow networks, background workflows, and longer waits.
general.networkDesc: Tune timeout and retry behavior so it matches your network conditions.
general.advancedNote: Timeout and retry settings affect perceived responsiveness. On stable networks, lower retry counts often feel better because failures surface faster.
general.closeToTrayNotice: YAT is still running in the system tray. Click the tray icon to reopen.
general.windowBehaviorDesc: Control close behavior, launch at login, and tray interactions.

stt.baseUrlHint: Use the root URL of an OpenAI-compatible API. YAT will append /audio/transcriptions automatically.
stt.apiKeyHintRemote: Remote providers usually require an API key.
stt.apiKeyHintLocal: Optional for localhost or private-network STT endpoints that do not require auth.
stt.modelHint: Enter the model name from your provider, e.g. whisper-large-v3-turbo
stt.quickStartDesc: Pick a stable endpoint, set the model, add a key if your service needs one, then run a quick connection test.
stt.connectionDesc: The base URL and model always need to be correct. API keys are optional for local no-auth endpoints.
stt.validationDesc: Send a short silent sample so you can verify the full STT request path end to end.
stt.validationSuccessBody: Your current STT settings responded successfully.

llm.enabledHint: When this is off, YAT outputs the raw transcript directly for maximum speed and predictability.
llm.apiKeyHintRemote: Remote providers usually require an API key.
llm.apiKeyHintLocal: Optional for localhost or private-network LLM endpoints that do not require auth.
llm.modelHint: Enter the model name from your provider, e.g. gpt-4o-mini
llm.quickStartTitle: Polishing is optional. Turn it on when you want steadier output
llm.quickStartDesc: Think of polishing as a second cleanup pass: punctuation, filler removal, and formatting. Leave it off if raw speed matters most.
llm.modeDesc: You do not need it every time. Turn it on when you want cleaner, steadier text.
llm.connectionDesc: YAT uses /chat/completions, so make sure your provider and model support that path. API keys are optional for local no-auth endpoints.
llm.bestUseBody: Turn this on when you want filler removal, punctuation, bullets, or more consistent wording.
llm.validationDesc: Send a minimal request so you can confirm the endpoint and API key both work.
llm.validationSuccessBody: Your current polishing settings responded successfully.
llm.disabledNoticeTitle: Raw transcription is being used directly
llm.disabledNoticeBody: You can still record and output text normally. Come back later if you want cleaner formatting and smarter cleanup.

prompt.userInstructionsDesc: Start with a few simple rules such as “keep the casual tone” or “turn lists into bullets.” Most cases do not need system prompt edits.
prompt.userInstructionsPlaceholder: e.g. Keep the casual tone, but preserve product names in English…
prompt.userInstructionsHint: Describe the output style you want before piling on more rules. Shorter and clearer is usually steadier.
prompt.systemPromptDesc: This is the lowest-level and strongest rule source. Only change it when you really know what you want to override.
prompt.llmDisabledBody: Prompt and vocabulary rules only matter when the LLM pass is enabled. If you just want fast raw output for now, leaving polishing off is fine.
prompt.simpleFirstBody: A few clear instructions usually work better than diving straight into advanced settings.
prompt.previewDesc: This combines the default system prompt, your custom instructions, and vocabulary rules so you can inspect what is actually sent.
prompt.advancedWarningTitle: Edit this only when you truly need to change the base rules
prompt.advancedWarningBody: The system prompt affects every output. Over-editing it often makes results less stable, not more. Try simple instructions first.

presets.keepToneValue: Keep the original tone. Do not over-formalize the writing.
presets.bulletsValue: If the content contains multiple items, turn them into a bullet list.
presets.formalValue: Make the text cleaner and clearer without changing the meaning.
presets.meetingValue: If the content sounds like tasks or updates, format it like concise meeting notes.

vocabulary.emptyHint: Add one common correction first and future output will be steadier.
vocabulary.safeCustomizationTitle: Vocabulary is the safest place to start
vocabulary.safeCustomizationBody: If you mainly want to fix brand names, product terms, team jargon, or common mistakes, vocabulary rules are usually safer than editing prompts directly.
vocabulary.addDesc: Each rule is applied during polishing, so this is a good place to build your own terminology list over time.
vocabulary.validationRequired: Please fill in both the wrong form and the correct form.
vocabulary.validationDuplicate: That wrong form already exists, so you do not need to add it again.
vocabulary.listDesc: The more complete this list is, the more stable proper nouns and fixed terms become.

history.emptyHint: Start recording and recent results will appear here.
history.confirmClearOld: Clear all expired entries? This cannot be undone.
history.summaryBody: When you want to copy, retry, or compare results, this screen should stay quick and easy to scan.

metrics.retentionHint: Entries older than this can be cleaned up regularly.

history.controlsDesc: Set the retention window, then search or clear older entries when needed.
history.resultsDesc: Each entry should be easy to copy, retry, or delete.

capsule.dismissedHint: Recording was too short and has been dismissed.
capsule.noSpeechHint: No speech was detected in this recording.
capsule.clipboardFallbackHint: Paste failed, but the text is still in your clipboard.
capsule.clipboardFallbackHintWindows: Paste failed, but the text is still in your clipboard. On Windows this often happens when the target app is running elevated or simulated input is blocked.
capsule.clipboardFallbackHintMacos: Paste failed, but the text is still in your clipboard. Make sure YAT has Accessibility permission and the target app accepts simulated input.
capsule.errorUnknown: Something went wrong, but no detailed reason was provided.
